title: Malthor
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Berren_Sid_Te.jpg]] |name=Malthor
{{C|Berren Sid Té in the streets of Fabrillan depicted}} |region=*[[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Malthor system/Legends|Malthor system]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Malthor system/Legends|Malthor system]] |sector=[[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]] |system=[[Malthor system/Legends|Malthor system]] |coordinates=Q-14 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain= |water= |interest=*[["Downside"]] *[[Fabrillan City Spaceport]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=*[[Human/Legends|Humans]] *[[Reigat]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population=At least c. 50,000 |demonym= |cities=[[Fabrillan]] |imports= |exports= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|The 'Spek-Man' is, perhaps, the strangest operative I've ever reviewed. He joined the Rebellion a few years ago in a 'part-time' fashion before Sid ever had help setting up the Sleeper Cell on Malthor.|Personal addendum by General Airen Cracken to an entry of a datafile detailing Rebel Alliance operatives dedicated to the "Spek-Man"|}} '''Malthor''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]]. It featured the active [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Fabrillan]], which in turn was the site of the busy [[Fabrillan City Spaceport]]. Fabrillan was home to a relatively large population of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reigat]], and the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Berren Sid Té]] and [[Reginna Bel Typolla]] were also [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the planet. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]–affiliated [[Sleeper Cell V-16]] of civilian operatives was established in Fabrillan, with its main focus being on information gathering and rendering assistance to any Rebel operatives visiting Malthor. At one point, Bel Typolla ventured off-world to participate in the [[Medepiest Uprising]], only for her to eventually return to Malthor, and the Reigat criminal [[Talak]] also resided in Fabrillan for some [[Time/Legends|time]]. ==Description== Malthor was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] situated in the [[Malthor system/Legends|Malthor system]], a part of the [[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. ==History== {{Quote|Gina is a native of Malthor, and she thinks we don't know that she spent some time running guns during the Medepiest Uprising. Fortunately, she survived that ill-timed battle and returned to her homeworld intact.|Personal addendum by General Airen Cracken to an entry of a datafile detailing Rebel Alliance operatives dedicated to Reginna Bel Typolla|}} [[File:Reginna_Bel_Typolla.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Malthor's native Reginna Bel Typolla returned to her homeworld after smuggling weapons during the Medepiest Uprising.]] Around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', which is set shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], gives [[Berren Sid Té]] and [[Reginna Bel Typolla]]'s age as forty-five and twenty-six, respectively, and also notes that [[Sleeper Cell V-16]] has existed for at least one [[Standard year/Legends|year]], which, per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', corresponds to [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of around [[45 BBY/Legends|45 BBY]] and [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]] as well as an approximate date of [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] for the establishment of Sleeper Cell V-16. during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] helped establish the Alliance-affiliated civilian [[Sleeper Cell V-16]] in Malthor's [[Fabrillan]] [[City/Legends|city]]. The sleeper cell was based on the [[Information broker/Legends|information gathering]] network led by the local street [[Merchant/Legends|vendor]] [[Berren Sid Té]], and its membership included Sid Té, the [[Bouncer (occupation)/Legends|bouncer]] [[Bakku]], the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Margo Flarestream]], the [[Fabrillan City Spaceport]] docking attendant [[Reginna Bel Typolla]], and the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Programmer/Legends|programmer]] known as the "[[Spek-Man]]." Besides information gathering, Sleeper Cell V-16 also aimed to assist any Alliance agents visiting Malthor, whom the civilian operatives would usually contact first. Among the specialized help the sleeper cell's members could provide, Flarestream was usually recruited for any transportation off-world that was required. At some point, Bel Typolla had traveled off Malthor to participate as a [[Smuggler/Legends|gunrunner]] in the [[Medepiest Uprising]] that shared its name with the [[Medepiest system/Legends|Medepiest system]] of the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Eventually, she safely returned to Malthor. In addition, [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] believed that the [[Reigat]] criminal [[Talak]], who was thought to have visited Malthor on multiple occasions, had resided in Fabrillan before hiring out his strength and combat abilities and ultimately traveling off-world.{{JournalCite|10|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} Not long after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] mentioned Malthor in several entries dedicated to Sleeper Cell V-16 that were part of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives. Cracken would also go on to mention the planet's role in Talak's biography in ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]{{JournalCite|1|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} establishes that the "[[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|Wanted by Cracken]]" segment of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' represents an [[in-universe]] update to the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', the original version of which is presented in the sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]. The latter work is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which corresponds to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. datafile that served as a compilation of reports on various threats to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] ==Inhabitants== Malthor's city of Fabrillan had a relatively large population of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Reigat, numbering approximately fifty thousand individuals. The settlement was also home to several [[Human/Legends|Humans]], with Malthor being the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Berren Sid Té and Reginna Bel Typolla, who were [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[45 BBY/Legends|45 BBY]] and [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]], respectively. In addition, the [[near-Human]] "Spek-Man," who was skilled at surviving in Malthor's "wilds," resided outside of Fabrillan. At one point, several [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] were also present in the city. ==Locations== Fabrillan was a bustling trade city on Malthor, hosting the busy Fabrillan City Spaceport as well as the slum area known as [["Downside"|"Downside."]] ==Behind the scenes== Malthor was created by [[Ed Stark]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, several scenes set on the planet were illustrated by [[Brian Schomburg]]. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Malthor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Malthor itself, in grid square Q-14. ==Sources== {{Indexpage}} {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Malthor}} *''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' {{1stm}} *{{JournalCite|10|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Planets in the Mid Rim]] [[Category:Planets in the Slice]] [[Category:Tandon sector locations]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Malthor locations]] }}
